methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Insitro is a Series C biotech company founded by Daphne Koller that has raised over $700M and carries a last post-money valuation of $2.4B. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, including Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and HistoSonics across similar development stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $2.4B Series C valuation, implying $4.1B. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.7B, with a range spanning $600M to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied as Insitro's revenue is not publicly disclosed, preventing meaningful P/S multiple application despite a 6.6x median P/S across 3 public biotech tickers.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $4.7B, with a median of $2.9B across the two applicable methods. The current $2.4B valuation sits below this midpoint. Key uncertainties include stage-progression timing and execution risk typical of Series C biotechs, as well as the wide peer dispersion ($600M–$4.0B), which reflects varied commercial maturity and clinical-stage profiles within the comparable set.
